marketFocus: Iran War Escalates — Larijani Killed, Missiles on Tel Aviv, Hormuz Won't Return to Pre-War Status: Iran's security chief Larijani was killed in a US/Israeli airstrike; Iran's Basij force commander Gholamreza Soleimani also killed. Iran retaliated by targeting Tel Aviv with missiles carrying cluster warheads. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf stated the Strait of Hormuz situation 'won't return to its pre-war status.' US Central Command struck Iranian missile sites along the Hormuz coastline with 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions. Russia confirmed it is providing Iran with satellite imagery and drone technology. Despite the escalation, oil partially retreated — WTI settled $2.71 higher at $96.21/bbl, Brent $3.21 higher at $103.42/bbl — as Iraq-Kurdistan reached an agreement to resume exports to Ceyhan port in Turkey, and the US moved to ease Venezuela oil sanctions.

Dollar Slips as Risk-On Prevails — DXY Softens to 99.52 Ahead of FOMC: The US FX Wrap headline was 'Dollar slips as risk-on prevails; AUD strength helped by hawkish RBA Governor Bullock press conference.' DXY waned from its 100.12 peak and fell below Monday's 99.66 low to a 99.52 trough, with price action largely mirroring oil markets. EUR/USD traded sideways near 1.1500 after German ZEW Economic Sentiment collapsed to -0.5 (from 58.3 prior) and EZ ZEW plunged to -8.5 (from 39.4 prior) — the sharpest single-month drop in the survey's history, reflecting the Iran war shock. GBP/USD paused near 1.3300 with UK Chancellor Reeves warning the Iran conflict 'is likely to put upward pressure on inflation in the months to come.' USD/JPY rangebound near 159.00 as the BOJ kicked off its 2-day policy meeting.

Super Wednesday: BOC (14:45 FFT), FOMC (19:00 FFT) + Powell Press Conference (19:30 FFT), NZD GDP (22:45 FFT): FOMC expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% and release updated dot plot projections. BOC decision is a coin-flip between hold and hike given the energy windfall vs -83.9k employment shock. NZD Current Account Q4 already missed (-5.98B vs -4.78B exp). UK Jobs Report this morning (08:00 FFT): Claimant Count, Average Earnings, Unemployment Rate. EZ CPI Final (Feb) and US PPI (Feb) also today. ASX 200 gained on tech, utilities, and real estate strength; Nikkei 225 climbed back above 55,000 on Japan trade surplus and US-Japan rare earths/AI deal. Money markets pricing a coin-flip for a third consecutive RBA hike in May.

USD.tone: Dollar Slipping — DXY 99.52, Risk-On Prevails, FOMC Hold Expected
USD.summary: The dollar slipped on Tuesday as risk-on sentiment prevailed despite the constant flow of Middle East headlines. DXY fell from its 100.12 peak to a 99.52 trough, with price action mirroring oil markets — as oil retreated, so did the safe-haven USD bid. The structural case for USD remains intact (Core PCE 3.1% YoY, Fed on hold, only 18bps of easing priced for 2026), but the near-term momentum has shifted bearish as the market prices a benign FOMC hold with no new hawkish catalyst. US PPI (Feb) at 13:30 FFT and FOMC decision at 19:00 FFT are the key catalysts today. A hawkish Powell press conference could reverse the dollar's slide; a dovish tone would accelerate it. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: DXY fell from 100.12 peak to 99.52 trough — price action mirroring oil markets
US FX Wrap: 'Dollar slips as risk-on prevails' — safe-haven bid fading as oil retreats
Core PCE 3.1% YoY structural case intact but near-term momentum shifted bearish
Only 18bps of Fed easing priced for 2026 — FOMC hold fully expected
US PPI (Feb) at 13:30 FFT + FOMC Decision at 19:00 FFT + Powell at 19:30 FFT

EUR.tone: ZEW Collapsed — German -0.5 vs 58.3 Prior, EUR/USD Capped Near 1.1500
EUR.summary: EUR/USD traded in a narrow 1.1466-1.1544 range on Tuesday, unable to hold any rally as the ZEW Economic Sentiment data confirmed the Iran war shock on European confidence. German ZEW Economic Sentiment collapsed to -0.5 from 58.3 prior — the sharpest single-month drop in the survey's history. EZ ZEW plunged to -8.5 from 39.4 prior. EUR edged higher in line with softer USD but saw downticks on the sub-par ZEW metrics. The EU moved to delay raising bank capital requirements over competitiveness concerns. EZ CPI Final (Feb) today and ECB tomorrow (14:15 FFT) with updated macro projections are the key catalysts. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: German ZEW Economic Sentiment -0.5 (from 58.3 prior) — sharpest monthly drop in history
EZ ZEW Economic Sentiment -8.5 (from 39.4 prior) — Iran war shock confirmed in data
EUR/USD 1.1466-1.1544 range — rally capped despite softer USD
EU delayed raising bank capital requirements over competitiveness concerns
EZ CPI Final (WED 11:00 FFT) + ECB Decision (THU 14:15 FFT) = key catalysts

GBP.tone: Stagflation Deepens — Reeves: Iran Conflict Will Push Inflation Higher, BOE Tomorrow
GBP.summary: GBP/USD traded in a 1.3273-1.3347 range on Tuesday, firmer alongside peers amid modest USD softness but with UK-specific newsflow confirming the stagflation narrative. UK Chancellor Reeves explicitly stated the Iran conflict 'is likely to put upward pressure on inflation in the months to come' — a direct acknowledgment that the BoE faces a worsening policy straitjacket. UK-US relations remain under pressure amid reported tensions over positioning in the Iran conflict (Trump: 'not happy with UK'). UK Jobs Report this morning (08:00 FFT): Claimant Count Change, Average Earnings Index 3m/y, and Unemployment Rate. BOE Meeting tomorrow (13:00 FFT). Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: GBP/USD 1.3273-1.3347 range — firmer on USD softness but capped by stagflation
Reeves: 'Iran conflict is likely to put upward pressure on inflation in months to come'
UK-US tensions: Trump 'not happy with UK'; UK-US relations under pressure
UK DMO plans new 10yr gilt in April + long-maturity gilt in June — supply pressure
UK Jobs Report (WED 08:00 FFT) + BOE Meeting (THU 13:00 FFT) = key catalysts

JPY.tone: BOJ 2-Day Meeting Started — Hold at 0.75% Expected, USD/JPY Rangebound 159.00
JPY.summary: USD/JPY remained rangebound near 159.00 on Tuesday, with the pair heavy amid broader USD softness and a steady decline in oil prices — but still well within the intervention zone. The BOJ kicked off its 2-day policy meeting on Tuesday; the central bank is unanimously forecast to hold at 0.75% with no policy tweaks. Japan trade data was mostly better than expected (Trade Balance +57.3B vs -1152.7B prior; Exports +4.2% YoY, Imports +10.2% YoY), and the Nikkei 225 climbed back above 55,000 on US-Japan rare earths and AI shipbuilding robot joint development deals. However, the structural bearish case remains: Japan imports 90%+ of crude from the Middle East, and the Hormuz closure is a direct terms-of-trade hit. BOJ decision and press conference tomorrow (04:00/07:30 FFT). Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY rangebound near 159.00 — heavy on USD softness + oil decline, but in intervention zone
BOJ 2-day meeting started — unanimously expected to hold at 0.75%, no policy tweaks
Japan Trade Balance +57.3B (prev. -1152.7B); Exports +4.2% YoY, Imports +10.2% YoY
Nikkei 225 back above 55,000 — US-Japan rare earths + AI shipbuilding robot deal
BOJ Decision (THU ~04:00 FFT) + Press Conference (THU 07:30 FFT) = key catalysts

AUD.tone: Post-RBA Hike Strength — AUD/USD Holding Gains, May Hike Coin-Flip
AUD.summary: AUD/USD held on to its post-RBA spoils on Tuesday as Antipodeans remained afloat amid the mostly positive risk environment. Governor Bullock's hawkish press conference (stressing inflation remains elevated and that dissent centered on timing rather than direction of further tightening) continued to underpin AUD. ASX 200 gained with strength in tech, utilities, and real estate — though gains were limited amid weakness in health care and consumer sectors following the rate hike. Money markets are now pricing a coin-flip for a third consecutive RBA hike in May. Australia's net energy exporter status remains a structural positive. AUD Employment Report tomorrow (01:30 FFT) is the next key catalyst. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: AUD/USD holding post-RBA hike gains — Bullock hawkish: 'timing not direction of tightening'
Money markets: coin-flip for third consecutive RBA hike in May
ASX 200 gained on tech, utilities, real estate — limited by health care + consumer weakness
Australia net energy exporter — structural positive vs EUR/JPY/GBP energy importers
AUD Employment Change + Unemployment Rate (THU 01:30 FFT) = next key catalyst

CAD.tone: Petro-Currency Bid — WTI $96.21/bbl, Iraq-Kurdistan Export Deal, BOC Today
CAD.summary: CAD remains supported by WTI at $96.21/bbl and Brent at $103.42/bbl, with the Iraq-Kurdistan agreement to resume exports to Ceyhan port in Turkey providing a partial supply offset. Canada is also supplying 24mln bbls as part of the IEA stabilisation effort. The US easing Venezuela oil sanctions to boost output is a mild negative for CAD (more supply), but the structural petro-currency bid is intact. BOC decision today at 14:45 FFT is the key catalyst — the debate is between the energy windfall (bullish CAD) and the -83.9k employment shock (bearish CAD). A hike would be strongly bullish; a hold with hawkish language would be mildly bullish. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI settled $96.21/bbl (+$2.71); Brent $103.42/bbl (+$3.21) — petro-currency bid intact
Iraq-Kurdistan agreement to resume Ceyhan port exports — partial supply offset
Canada supplying 24mln bbls as part of IEA stabilisation effort
US easing Venezuela oil sanctions — mild negative (more supply) but CAD still supported
BOC Rate Decision (WED 14:45 FFT) + Press Conference (WED 15:30 FFT) = key catalyst

CHF.tone: Haven vs Energy Importer — SNB Tomorrow, EUR/CHF Stable
CHF.summary: CHF remains in a tug-of-war between haven demand from the escalating Iran war and Switzerland's energy import dependence. EUR/CHF was stable as both currencies are treated as energy-short assets. Swiss Producer & Import Prices YoY fell -2.7% (prev. -2.2%) and MoM -0.3% — deflationary pressure from the energy shock on input costs, but the Franc's haven appeal is partially offsetting. SNB meets tomorrow at 09:30 FFT — Chairman Schlegel's tone on imported inflation vs currency strength will be the key signal. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: EUR/CHF stable — both currencies treated as energy-short assets
Swiss PPI YoY -2.7% (prev. -2.2%), MoM -0.3% — deflationary input cost pressure
Haven demand from Iran war escalation partially offsets energy importer vulnerability
SNB Meeting (THU 09:30 FFT) + Press Conference (THU 10:00 FFT) = key catalyst
Schlegel tone on imported inflation vs CHF strength = directional signal for CHF

NZD.tone: Current Account Miss — NZD Q4 -5.98B vs -4.78B Exp, GDP Tonight 22:45 FFT
NZD.summary: NZD/USD was mixed overnight after Tuesday's session, with AUD continuing to outperform as the RBA hike story dominates the Antipodean narrative. New Zealand Current Account Q4 missed at -5.98B vs -4.78B expected (prev. -8.37B) — a wider deficit than forecast, adding to the bearish case. The Trump-Xi summit delay (by one month due to the Iran war) is a negative signal for NZD via the China demand channel. NZD GDP Q4 tonight at 22:45 FFT is the only domestic catalyst this week — a miss would accelerate the bearish case. No RBNZ meeting until April. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: NZD Current Account Q4 -5.98B (vs -4.78B exp, prev. -8.37B) — wider deficit than forecast
Trump-Xi summit delayed by one month due to Iran war — negative for NZD via China channel
AUD outperforming on hawkish RBA — AUD/NZD divergence widening
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
NZD GDP Q4 (WED 22:45 FFT) = only domestic catalyst; miss would accelerate bearish case
